definition: API Design refers to the process of creating and defining the architecture of an Application Programming Interface (API), which enables different software systems to communicate with each other. It is also known as API Architecture or API Development.
simple_explanation: Think of API Design like designing a restaurant's menu and ordering system. The menu represents the services the restaurant offers, and the ordering system represents how customers can request those services. In API Design, you define what services your system offers and how other systems can request them, making it easy for different systems to work together.
technical_explanation: Under the hood, API Design involves defining the API's endpoints, request and response formats, authentication mechanisms, and error handling protocols. Key mechanisms include RESTful APIs, API gateways, and service discovery, which enable scalable, secure, and maintainable API architectures. Engineering trade-offs in API Design involve balancing factors like performance, security, and usability to create an efficient and reliable interface for system integration.
investor_explanation: Robust configuration of API Design directly affects system efficiency and business-critical accuracy metrics, driving cost savings and higher end-user retention.
examples: We optimized API Design in our AI Engineering pipeline to improve performance.
Our team discussed the differences between API Design and alternative solutions.
Understanding API Design is highly beneficial when scaling our System Design capabilities.
interview_questions: How does API Design fit into standard AI Engineering architectures?
What are the major engineering trade-offs when implementing API Design?
How do you debug common failure modes associated with API Design?
common_mistakes: Misconfiguring parameters for API Design, resulting in degraded quality.
Underestimating the computational cost or memory footprint of API Design.
